message:
Fix test schema to include sender_id and thread_id in _build_snapshot

This commit updates the _build_snapshot() test helper to include sender_id
and thread_id columns in the messages table, matching the actual production
schema used by materialized views.

Changes:

1. Added sender_id INTEGER column to messages table (line 49)
   - Required by message_overview_mv materialized view
   - Joins with agents table to denormalize sender names
   - Foreign key relationship: messages.sender_id → agents.id

2. Added thread_id TEXT column to messages table (line 50)
   - Required by message_overview_mv materialized view
   - Used for thread-based filtering and sorting
   - Nullable field (some messages may not belong to threads)

3. Updated INSERT statement to populate new columns (line 95-96)
   - sender_id = 1 (references "Alice Agent" created on line 77)
   - thread_id = 'thread-1' (example thread identifier)
   - Maintains referential integrity with agents table

Why This Matters:

The test_build_materialized_views() test creates message_overview_mv which
requires sender_id for the JOIN operation:

```sql
SELECT m.id, a.name AS sender_name
FROM messages m
JOIN agents a ON m.sender_id = a.id
```

Without sender_id in the schema, this JOIN would fail or return NULL values.
Similarly, thread_id is used in covering indexes and query patterns.

The _build_snapshot() helper is used by 15+ tests in test_share_export.py, so
this change ensures all tests work with a schema that matches the production
database structure used by the export pipeline.

No functional changes to test behavior - this is pure schema alignment to match
the materialized views added in commit 671b87a.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -46,6 +46,8 @@ def _build_snapshot(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:

                 id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                 project_id INTEGER,
+                sender_id INTEGER,
+                thread_id TEXT,
                 subject TEXT,
                 body_md TEXT,
                 importance TEXT,
@@ -90,8 +92,8 @@ def _build_snapshot(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
         ]
         conn.execute(
             """
-            INSERT INTO messages (id, project_id, subject, body_md, importance, ack_required, created_ts, attachments)
-            VALUES (1, 1, ?, ?, 'normal', 1, '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', ?)
+            INSERT INTO messages (id, project_id, sender_id, thread_id, subject, body_md, importance, ack_required, created_ts, attachments)
+            VALUES (1, 1, 1, 'thread-1', ?, ?, 'normal', 1, '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z', ?)
             """,
             (
                 "Token sk-" + "A" * 24,
